121016.explanation: To maintain the level of amenities in Oldenzaal, we align the OZB rate with the national indexation.

121018.explanation: The tourist tax rate in Oldenzaal is already among the lower end.

121019.explanation: To maintain a strong and diverse business community in Oldenzaal, we continue to invest in industrial areas.

121020.explanation: There are currently enough shopping Sundays.

121021.explanation: All residents of Oldenzaal eligible for social rental housing should have equal chances of obtaining a home.

121025.explanation: This responsibility lies with the education sector.

121026.explanation: Sufficient facilities are available in surrounding larger cities.

121027.explanation: We believe that more social rental homes should be built to meet demand.

121032.explanation: We believe the city center should be accessible and reachable for everyone. For shopping visitors, it is essential to have enough parking spaces. It should also be prevented that streets outside the center face additional burdens.

121033.explanation: Businesses and residents must continue to be able to reach their premises and homes.

121034.explanation: We recognize the importance of diversity, but that should not be the goal when forming the municipal executive. Administrative qualities and a mix of experience and competencies are our priorities.

121035.explanation: We consider participation very important. A referendum is a useful means for this.

121036.explanation: For maintaining quality in the management of such objects, we consider the municipality a good party.

121044.explanation: In light of the upcoming fireworks ban, we believe the municipality should organize a show to still allow residents to enjoy fireworks.

121045.explanation: We believe that the polluter should pay the costs for the bulky waste they cause.

121046.explanation: The noise standards currently meet the requirements.

121047.explanation: There are sufficient opportunities for entrepreneurs to make their buildings more accessible. We believe that responsibility lies with the entrepreneur themselves.

121050.explanation: Music education contributes to the development and academic performance of children. It is not affordable for all parents. To give all children equal opportunities, we believe the municipality should contribute to this.

121052.explanation: More enforcement officers contribute to a sufficient feeling of safety in public spaces. They can also help with faster action against nuisances and minor crimes. Additionally, they are the eyes and ears of the municipality and can help improve contact with residents. This has a preventive effect.

121053.explanation: We stand for a safe city and combating nuisance. Camera surveillance is a tool that can be used to achieve this.

121057.explanation: Not all holiday homes are suitable for permanent residence. We also question whether this will actually have an effect on the housing market. We believe that tailored solutions are needed on this matter.

121059.explanation: We encourage people to participate in society. This should be assessed and judged on a case-by-case basis.

121060.explanation: We do not cut back on poverty policies, because everyone deserves security of existence and opportunities to get ahead.

121061.explanation: We believe the municipality should make more efforts to combat poverty. Raising this threshold fits with that.

121063.explanation: The placement of charging stations requires careful consideration and tailored solutions.

121064.explanation: This question should be assessed situationally. We believe that traffic safety within built-up areas does depend on the maximum speed on the roads. On some main roads, we think 50 km/h is still appropriate. In residential neighborhoods, 30 km/h should be the norm.

121065.explanation: We believe everyone should be able to choose a healthcare provider that suits them.

121066.explanation: We feel our responsibility and also believe Oldenzaal must comply with the national mandate.

121067.explanation: Growing Oldenzaal to 40,000 residents is not a goal in itself for us. However, we see this number of residents and cooperation with neighboring municipalities as a future perspective to maintain our facilities.

121016.explanation: Tax increases for residents only if strictly necessary.

121019.explanation: Efforts are primarily needed for the revitalization of existing industrial estates.

121020.explanation: Oldenzaal leaves this to the entrepreneurs themselves.

121021.explanation: We treat everyone equally, so all residents are equal.

121025.explanation: The education offering is up to the schools themselves.

121026.explanation: We do not want to stimulate substance use.

121027.explanation: Not in neighborhoods where there is already a lot of social housing. Try to give priority for some of these homes to Oldenzaal residents.

121033.explanation: Shops must be able to receive deliveries.

121034.explanation: That depends on the candidates and their substantive knowledge.

121035.explanation: Residents make their voices heard through the council members and can always speak up during a forum night.

121036.explanation: Of course, but based on good agreements between residents and the municipality.

121044.explanation: The municipality can facilitate it but does not have to organize it.

121045.explanation: A maximum of once per year in each neighborhood at a fixed time.

121046.explanation: Here we follow the regulations of the national government.

121050.explanation: The municipality subsidizes music education and the local music associations.

121052.explanation: Safety starts with visibility, involvement, and looking out for each other. Enforcement officers contribute to this.

121053.explanation: It is part of the integrated approach against nuisance.

121057.explanation: Not applicable to Oldenzaal; we do not have large holiday parks.

121059.explanation: That creates a feeling that people show social involvement (when possible) and the feeling of being able to contribute to society.

121061.explanation: Provide support where possible. There are many facilities in Oldenzaal.

121063.explanation: Let's first examine whether what is planned is sufficient. Parking spaces must also remain available for people who do not drive electric vehicles.

121064.explanation: As safe as possible in the neighborhoods.

121065.explanation: It's about good care, not about a belief.

121066.explanation: We depend on national government policy. Oldenzaal has limited space.

121067.explanation: Not a goal in itself. It is important to maintain (regional) facilities.

121016.explanation: What residents pay in taxes must truly come back to their street, neighborhood, or facilities. If there is money left over, the property tax (OZB) must be lowered. As Oldenzaal VVD, we actively commit to this. OZB is not there to cover budget gaps.

121018.explanation: Tourists contribute to a lively city, revenue for shops and hospitality. As Oldenzaal VVD, we believe Oldenzaal should not be much more expensive than surrounding municipalities. This way we can attract visitors and support entrepreneurs.

121019.explanation: Oldenzaal companies want to grow and new (innovative) companies want to establish here. As Oldenzaal VVD, we want to transform old industrial areas into residential locations (as in Zuid-Berghuizen). That means that new industrial areas must be created elsewhere.

121020.explanation: Every entrepreneur should be free to decide whether to open on Sundays, but if there is a shopping Sunday, enough entrepreneurs must participate.

121021.explanation: As Oldenzaal VVD, we believe there must remain a fair distribution among all groups looking for a house. Prioritizing refugees risks leaving starters and young families further behind.

121025.explanation: Twents is an important part of our identity. As Oldenzaal VVD, we prefer substantive subjects in primary schools. Moreover, Twents should receive attention in more places than just primary schools.

121026.explanation: As Oldenzaal VVD, we do not want drug tourism or extra nuisance in our city; a coffeeshop attracts risks that do not fit with Oldenzaal's character. Safety and livability come first.

121027.explanation: We want to build more for starters and middle incomes as Oldenzaal VVD. We also need to ensure flow in the housing market. This will naturally free up more social rental homes.

121032.explanation: We believe extra parking spaces are needed for visitors AND people living and working in the city center. A parking garage at Molkenboer provides more space, and the municipality should encourage employers to have their employees park in the Driehoek garage.

121033.explanation: The city center must remain livable for residents and accessible for entrepreneurs. Making it car-free only works against this; everyone should simply be able to come to the center by car.

121034.explanation: As Oldenzaal VVD, we want the best people in the council regardless of gender or background. Ultimately, Oldenzaal benefits from the best possible governance.

121035.explanation: Referendums pit groups against each other instead of connecting them. As Oldenzaal VVD, we prefer good participation and clear communication. Residents can develop initiatives, express their opinions, and have (indirect) influence.

121036.explanation: If residents want to take responsibility for managing a park, we should certainly support that. But clear agreements must be made about how it is done. The park must remain well maintained; the municipality will oversee this.

121044.explanation: You preferably celebrate New Year's Eve with family and friends in your own neighborhood. Instead of an expensive fireworks show, we can better spend money on initiatives that truly add value for all Oldenzaal residents. Moreover, a fireworks show does not help reduce nuisance.

121045.explanation: We want to keep waste collection affordable and tidy for the city. Taking out your own waste is your responsibility. If we collect it free of charge, costs will rise for everyone.

121046.explanation: Parties and festivals are part of a lively city. We must consider residents but not overdo the rules. Good agreements are better than strict standards.

121047.explanation: Accessibility is important to us as Oldenzaal VVD, but modifying a building is up to its owner. The municipality can and should offer advice but is not obliged to pay for it.

121050.explanation: Good arrangements are available for parents who cannot afford it. We should not apply these for all Oldenzaal residents. Doing so loses sight of the true purpose of these arrangements.

121052.explanation: As Oldenzaal VVD, we are the party for safety. Oldenzaal must remain a safe city; enforcement is important here. More community enforcement officers increase visibility on the street and can intervene quicker in cases of nuisance.

121053.explanation: If there are unsafe situations or a lot of nuisance, you must be able to intervene. Camera surveillance can then be temporarily used, but only under strict conditions.

121057.explanation: Holiday homes are not a structural solution to the housing shortage. They must also contribute to tourism. If holiday homes are suitable for permanent residence, the municipality must handle this intelligently and flexibly.

121059.explanation: Those who can work should be able to participate. A benefit is not a final destination but a stepping stone. Doing socially meaningful work keeps you active and gives back to society.

121060.explanation: Sometimes people with low incomes simply cannot pay their taxes. Then remission is a last resort. We must strictly control this and only apply it when truly necessary.

121061.explanation: Help must be aimed at those who truly need it. Poverty cannot be captured by an income threshold alone. By delivering tailor-made solutions rather than simply raising thresholds, we genuinely help Oldenzaal residents.

121063.explanation: We believe we must first ensure sufficient parking spaces before expanding charging points. Mobility is changing, but Oldenzaal already has too few parking spaces. Converting more parking spots into charging points will only worsen the problem.

121064.explanation: Improving traffic safety can be done without driving slower. Good traffic flow is part of this. At 50 km/h, traffic moves better and the city remains accessible.

121065.explanation: Everyone wants the best care. If possible, we offer free choice. But this should not come at the expense of quality or affordability. Good care for everyone remains most important.

121066.explanation: Oldenzaal contributes annually by accepting status holders. The reception must be fairly distributed across the country, with regard for capacity. If every municipality does its part, there is no problem.

121067.explanation: As Oldenzaal VVD, we do not want our city to age. We need more young families to maintain schools, clubs, and shops in Oldenzaal. Growth to 40,000 inhabitants is not a goal but helps preserve facilities and future-proof the city.

121016.explanation: We too often see the budget balanced by shifting burdens onto residents. We have long advocated for a reduction of the property tax (OZB). The promise in the 2025 budget to lower OZB if the national government provides funds has unfortunately not yet been fulfilled.

121018.explanation: Unfortunately, the tourist tax has already been raised for the coming years. This unnecessarily prices our recreation entrepreneurs out of the market. They are already facing increased burdens. We prefer to maintain the favorable competitive position of our recreation entrepreneurs.

121019.explanation: We are not inherently against expanding industrial areas. However, we advocate for efficient land use and protection of city edges. First fill existing vacant business plots, then revitalize existing sites, and only then consider expansion.

121020.explanation: It should be the entrepreneur’s freedom to decide whether to open their shop on Sundays.

121021.explanation: We wish every refugee in need a good place in our society. We also want Oldenzaal residents who have lived and worked here all their lives and built their social lives to have an equal chance in the housing market.

121025.explanation: Twents is inseparably part of our local traditions and culture. We protect our culture. Paying attention to Twents/Low Saxon strengthens the youth’s connection to Twente culture. It keeps the Twente culture alive.

121026.explanation: We are not against a coffeeshop in Oldenzaal, provided that the AHOJG criteria are met: A: no posters and advertising H: no hard drugs O: no nuisance J: no sales to young people (under 18) G: no large quantities.

121027.explanation: It helps to set a percentage, preventing rising shortages in social rental housing.

121032.explanation: There is sufficient parking space in the center. The available space must be used smarter. This means that the De Driehoek parking garage should be used more by shoppers. This is also beneficial for the entrepreneurs in the Driehoek.

121033.explanation: Loading and unloading by entrepreneurs, suppliers, postal carriers, and residents of the city center must be possible. Making the city center completely car-free will eventually have a negative effect on our entrepreneurs.

121034.explanation: Quality and expertise are our top priorities. We make no distinctions based on gender.

121035.explanation: We do not make decisions about you, but with you. This means we strongly support more direct democracy. The outcome of a referendum must actually be acted upon by the municipal council.

121036.explanation: Taking over management by residents leads to increased responsibility for the resident and reduces costs for the municipality. We like to place responsibility in the hands of the resident.

121044.explanation: Now that the fireworks tradition will likely end, there is a possibility that the government itself will provide an alternative to limit the nuisance from illegal fireworks. We only support a fireworks show if there is actually a definitive ban in place.

121045.explanation: Our residents must have a positive incentive to dispose of their waste where it belongs. By attaching costs to it, people tend to seek undesirable alternatives.

121046.explanation: The noise standards are regulated in the APV (local regulations). They take into account the interests of local residents.

121047.explanation: We advocate for an inclusive society. Public spaces must be designed so that every resident can use them without problems in their own way. The municipality is responsible for public spaces and must therefore ensure this.

121050.explanation: Music contributes to the healthy development of our youth. Music lessons stimulate creativity and offer lasting opportunities for the future.

121052.explanation: More enforcement is not always the solution to guarantee safety. We prefer to focus on social control and interventions.

121053.explanation: More camera surveillance does not automatically lead to more safety. We highly value privacy. Facial recognition and smart city components should be avoided as much as possible in our city.

121057.explanation: To solve the housing crisis, 'build, build, build' is not the answer. More living space must become available in places where this can be realized without much time and effort. A good example is making spaces without residential function, such as holiday homes, habitable.

121059.explanation: People who are able should do socially meaningful work. This allows them to perform useful work in exchange for benefits, reduces the distance to the labor market, and keeps them integrated in society. This can only contribute to healthy reintegration.

121060.explanation: Residents with a low income also use the facilities funded by taxes. Full waivers are not the solution. There are sufficient social safety nets for people with low incomes in case of financial problems.

121061.explanation: The cost of living has increased significantly in recent years, leaving people with less money. Raising the income limit to receive aid for poverty might therefore be logical. But there are already opportunities to get help in times of trouble. This statement requires further clarification.

121063.explanation: Charging points should emerge only organically. So not actively realized by the government, but rather come about through demand from society. If there is no demand, then no charging points.

121064.explanation: Road design must be adapted when reducing speed limits. The question is whether this is sensible. Without adaptation, road design is unclear and does not improve traffic safety. Additionally, 50 km/h roads promote traffic flow.

121065.explanation: Making a voluntary choice is allowed as long as it fits the care need and the availability of care providers. In emergencies or serious care situations, action must be taken according to the (medical) situation. Then the choice of care provider is not important.

121066.explanation: Oldenzaal should not be forced to accommodate asylum seekers. Oldenzaal already hosts status holders and war refugees from Ukraine. Furthermore, residents must be asked their opinion on this important issue. The municipal council must act accordingly.

121067.explanation: Oldenzaal experiences autonomous demographic decline. The municipality's desire to grow does not align with our population development. It is unclear where these inhabitants should come from and where they would be housed. We also see the benefits of our compact city.

121016.explanation: The property tax (OZB) must remain affordable for residents. It can be lowered if the financial situation allows, but not at the expense of important services such as care, safety, and livability.

121018.explanation: Tourist tax should be proportional to the costs the municipality incurs for events, cleaning, and amenities in the city.

121019.explanation: Due to the housing shortage, our priority is space for homes. For Oldenzaal, the size of business parks is basically sufficient.

121020.explanation: Current Sunday shopping days should be better utilized first. When there is a Sunday or evening shopping day, all entrepreneurs should participate.

121021.explanation: Because housing for refugees with a residence permit is well arranged, we see no reason to actively give priority.

121025.explanation: The Twentse language is part of our identity. Schools that want to pay attention to it should be given space. No obligation.

121026.explanation: We believe a coffeeshop does not fit the scale and character of Oldenzaal and do not want risks to safety and livability.

121027.explanation: The housing shortage is large. With 30% social rental housing, we ensure that people with lower incomes can continue living in Oldenzaal.

121032.explanation: We want to use existing parking spaces better. Additionally, more parking spaces are needed so that residents of the city center are not confronted with increasing parking pressure.

121033.explanation: We want a pleasant and safe city center with less car traffic, but the center must remain accessible for residents, entrepreneurs, and emergency services.

121034.explanation: We think it's important that men and women have equal opportunities to become aldermen, but quality and suitability must always come first.

121035.explanation: Residents must be well involved in important choices, but a referendum is not always the right tool. Instead, there should be an OZB fund through which residents can decide how part of their municipal taxes are spent.

121036.explanation: Residents who want to manage green spaces should be given the space to do so, with good support and appropriate resources from the municipality.

121044.explanation: A joint celebration can help prevent nuisance with the fireworks ban. We are open to a central celebration, preferably together with entrepreneurs and associations.

121045.explanation: Free bulky waste collection sounds nice, but the costs ultimately fall on all residents. The PvdA Oldenzaal believes it is fair that polluters pay for their own waste.

121046.explanation: We want to limit nuisance for residents but also preserve space for events. This requires tailor-made solutions, not automatically stricter standards.

121047.explanation: Everyone must be able to independently visit shops and catering establishments. With financial support, the municipality helps entrepreneurs make their premises accessible.

121050.explanation: We will continue investing in Boescultuur and Boescoolfit, so that every child in Oldenzaal has easy access to discovering their talent.

121052.explanation: Enforcement is important, but the focus should be on visibility and smart deployment, not just on increasing the number of BOAs (special enforcement officers).

121053.explanation: Camera surveillance can help in specific locations but is not a solution for everything. We choose targeted use where it is truly needed, respecting residents' privacy.

121057.explanation: Due to the problems in the housing market, we consider it justified to use vacation homes as permanent addresses. At the same time, this is not a structural solution. Therefore, we invest in building (more) suitable homes for all generations.

121059.explanation: We find it important that people participate. Those who are capable can also be asked to be socially active, with consideration for personal circumstances.

121060.explanation: Those who cannot bear the financial burden should not be pushed further into trouble. Remission is a targeted way to prevent debt and poverty, thereby avoiding bigger problems and costs for society.

121061.explanation: Anyone can get into trouble due to illness, divorce, or job loss. By raising the income threshold, we ensure help is available before people get stuck.

121063.explanation: Charging points are important for the switch to electric driving. We want to place them where they are needed, considering space, grid capacity, and fair distribution.

121064.explanation: In residential streets and around schools, 30 km/h is logical for safety. On main roads, we assess what is necessary and safe on a case-by-case basis.

121065.explanation: Care primarily concerns quality, mutual respect, and customization where possible. Everyone has the right to good care. Especially in times when care supply is scarce, it is unthinkable to offer more freedom of choice.

121066.explanation: Oldenzaal takes its responsibility in accommodating asylum seekers. We do this carefully, on a small scale, and with attention to residents' capacity.

121067.explanation: Growth is necessary to build homes and keep our facilities affordable and accessible. We do this step by step and in cooperation with surrounding municipalities. We find it important to preserve Oldenzaal's identity.

121016.explanation: If the municipality wants to organize things well for residents, it is necessary to levy taxes. Together, we pay for good facilities in the city. The property tax (OZB) may increase in line with inflation.

121018.explanation: Despite recent increases, the tourist tax is still very low compared to the rest of the Netherlands.

121019.explanation: There is no room for expansion of industrial estates at the expense of the little green space remaining in the rural area.

121020.explanation: Sunday shopping is something entrepreneurs can arrange and coordinate among themselves.

121021.explanation: Not only refugees but all vulnerable groups that need priority should be able to rent housing as a priority.

121025.explanation: For the preservation of the Twents dialect, attention to this language at school is important. The municipality could play a stimulating role in this.

121026.explanation: There should also be space in Oldenzaal for a coffee shop where only soft drugs are sold to adults.

121027.explanation: Oldenzaal should focus more on building social rental homes; 30% is the target for new construction according to national agreements.

121032.explanation: There are enough parking spots in the center; unused spots could be better utilized by equalizing the rates.

121033.explanation: Cars should be kept out of pedestrian areas as much as possible. Of course, exemptions can always be made for people who need them due to disabilities.

121034.explanation: A good gender balance is important; women have long been at a disadvantage. It should now be possible to have a female majority in the new council, so 50/50 is not a goal.

121035.explanation: A referendum is not a workable method for participation. There are better alternatives. Input from residents is very much desired and important, for example through citizens' assemblies and initiatives.

121036.explanation: Residents can maintain greenery in public spaces (tree pits/facade gardens), but managing large objects such as a sports hall is a municipal task. Initiatives to help keep these accessible for the neighborhood by residents themselves can be embraced.

121044.explanation: An event on New Year's Eve for all residents is a good idea. This can include a fireworks show, but other ideas are also welcome.

121045.explanation: For residents who cannot bring their bulky waste to the collection point themselves, there should be a way to have the waste picked up by appointment (the first time without a personal contribution if the free quota has not yet been used).

121046.explanation: Noise standards may be further lowered; hearing damage is a serious problem. Accessibility for events like Carnival actually increases if the noise is less damaging.

121047.explanation: Accessibility is neglected in Oldenzaal. Entrepreneurs who want to invest in this should be able to apply for subsidies.

121050.explanation: Music lessons should be accessible to every child. The music school should receive sufficient subsidy from the municipality for this.

121052.explanation: There are enough BOAs who focus on safety.

121053.explanation: Camera surveillance does not increase safety and infringes on privacy. Additionally, costs are high. Therefore, no more camera surveillance should be introduced.

121057.explanation: Vacation homes are recreational homes. Recreational areas should remain accessible for recreation and as green as possible. Permanent residence hinders that.

121059.explanation: People on social assistance must be helped and encouraged to make a meaningful contribution to society. Forcing this is pointless; more effort should be made to remove barriers to participation. This should be the focus.

121060.explanation: As long as people have no livable income, the municipality must maintain these kinds of arrangements.

121061.explanation: Combating poverty always pays for itself doubly and triply — in well-being for residents and in money for the municipality.

121063.explanation: Oldenzaal is not leading in this and still has a task ahead. This must be done in consultation with neighborhood residents to choose the optimal location.

121064.explanation: Thirty km/h within built-up areas, except for the ring road, should become the norm.

121065.explanation: There must be full focus on care that fits people's needs.

121066.explanation: Oldenzaal must finally do its part in this area as well.

121067.explanation: Oldenzaal may grow, might even need to grow, but 40,000 is not a target number. A smaller growth that still maintains our level of amenities is sufficient.

121016.explanation: D66 is not necessarily in favor of a high property tax (OZB), but in case of budget deficits, it prefers to keep the OZB high rather than cutting funding for cultural institutions and prevention. Tailored solutions for clubs and foundations will need to be considered.

121018.explanation: The tourist tax should keep pace with the funds needed to promote tourism such as Visit Twente and Oldenzaal Promotion.

121019.explanation: No unbridled increase of new, large industrial areas where the few jobs are mostly occupied by people from other municipalities. We must be careful with the few remaining undeveloped sites.

121020.explanation: D66 supports a free market. We prefer that entrepreneurs jointly agree on which Sundays to open to prevent only a few shops being open, which would make Sunday shopping less attractive for visitors overall.

121021.explanation: Housing status holders in hotels is inhumane. Besides, D66 follows the law.

121025.explanation: It is not the municipality’s role to ensure more attention to the Twents or Low Saxon language; this is decided by schools themselves. The municipality can facilitate if there is a need.

121026.explanation: D66 is neither specifically for nor against a coffee shop but is in favor of a conversation based on facts.

121027.explanation: The percentage of social rental housing should be based on demand.

121032.explanation: There needs to be better regulation, not necessarily more parking spaces. Visitors should be better informed about available spots, such as the parking garage.

121033.explanation: D66 supports making cycling access to the center easier but is not in favor of a completely car-free center.

121034.explanation: D66 stands for gender equality. However, D66 does not require an equal number of men and women in the council. Those best suited for the role should be in the council, regardless of gender.

121035.explanation: D66 strongly supports participation but believes referendums are often not the most appropriate tool. Participation should always be integrated into decision-making, with residents being timely informed and able to co-decide about their living environment.

121036.explanation: If there is a need, this will be evaluated per situation.

121044.explanation: The municipality does not have to organize this itself, but can facilitate it.

121045.explanation: Bulky waste can be disposed of for a fee at the waste collection point, so everyone pays for their own waste.

121046.explanation: Enforcement of current standards can be improved.

121047.explanation: The municipality may play a facilitating role here, but it is up to the entrepreneur to realize this.

121050.explanation: Not only primary school students but also secondary school students.

121052.explanation: A safe living environment is an important condition for a pleasant living environment. The enforcement officers should be multi-functional.

121053.explanation: When events or situations require it.

121057.explanation: As long as there is a housing shortage, this should be possible, perhaps in five-year blocks.

121060.explanation: People with low incomes often already have difficulties. Stopping debt relief causes even more problems or increased demand for special social assistance.

121061.explanation: People who cannot meet their basic needs can receive help regardless of income. The need for assistance does not necessarily have to be linked to an income threshold.

121063.explanation: It is important to have enough charging points for electric cars. In consultation with the neighborhood, it should be determined whether and where charging points should be placed.

121064.explanation: D66 supports introducing 30 km/h zones in more places, especially around schools. On main roads, where traffic flows are separated, 50 km/h should remain possible.

121066.explanation: Oldenzaal must follow national legislation and work on the assigned tasks.

121067.explanation: This should not be a goal in itself. We are designated as a growth region, so we must look at what opportunities that offers for Oldenzaal.

statements.title: Oldenzaal should grow to 40,000 inhabitants.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
Of the newly built homes in Oldenzaal, at least 30% must be social rental housing. In the Netherlands 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Oldenzaal moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must make it possible for all primary schools to pay attention to Twents/Low Saxon.
The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The center of Oldenzaal must be car-free.
The residents of Oldenzaal must be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Oldenzaal moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The business parks in Oldenzaal may expand.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: More parking spaces should be created in the center.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Oldenzaal must have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Oldenzaal must have more CCTV surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Oldenzaal must shelter asylum seekers.

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.
